title: श्री साई चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री साई चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥

प्रथम साईं के चरण में, करता मस्तक टेक।
सकल मनोरथ पूर्ण कर, रखना अपनी शेक॥

जय साईं जय साईं जय, करुणासागर साईं।
भक्तन हित अवतार लिय, सब सुख देत सदाईं॥

॥ चौपाई ॥

शिर्डी नगर विराजे साईं। कृपा करो दयानिधि भाईं॥
चरण शरण में हमें रखो तुम। प्रेम भक्ति से तुम्हें भजूँ मैं॥ १॥

पतित पावन तुम हो दाता। सकल जगत के हो विधाता॥
भक्त हेतु तुम शिर्डी आये। चमत्कार बहु दिखलाये॥ २॥

जल को पलटत तेल बनाया। अचरज सबने बहुत मनाया॥
भक्तों को तुम दर्शन देते। उनके सकल कष्ट हर लेते॥ ३॥

श्रद्धा सबुरी बताई तुमने। दो शब्दों में ज्ञान सिखाया॥
करुणा निधि तुम दीनदयालू। भक्त हेतु सदा प्रतिपालू॥ ४॥

द्वारकामाई तुम्हारा वासा। पूर्ण करो सबकी मन आशा॥
धूनी साईं तुम्हारी जलती। विपदा भक्तन की हर बलती॥ ५॥

मुसलमान कह जो तुम पुकारे। हिन्दू कहकर जो तुम्हें निहारे॥
दोनों की रखवारी करते। भेद भाव से कभी न डरते॥ ६॥

तुम हो सबके सच्चे साईं। निर्धन हो या धनवान भाईं॥
बाबा तुम्हें मनाऊँ कैसे। प्रेम प्रीत का दान दो ऐसे॥ ७॥

साईं तुम्हारा ही आसरा है। जग में तुम सा नहीं कोई खरा है॥
जो तेरे चरणों में आये। वो कभी खाली नहीं जाये॥ ८॥

मान अपमान तुम्हें नहीं बाबा। जग में तेरा अद्भुत ताबा॥
कुष्ठ रोग जिनको हो जाये। बाबा तेरी उदी लगाये॥ ९॥

सब रोगों से मुक्ति हो जाये। बाबा की कृपा बरसाये॥
भीमा बाई की सेवा कीन्हा। ताहि कंचन तन दीन्हा॥ १०॥

रोग मिटाये भक्तन के तुम। दुख दारिद्र्य भगाये सब तुम॥
मुरली मनोहर रूप तुम्हारा। गोधन चरावत अति प्यारा॥ ११॥

भक्तों को दर्शन दे जाते। अपने प्रेम में सबको बाँधे॥
बाबा तुम हो अन्तर्यामी। पूर्ण ब्रह्म परमात्मा स्वामी॥ १२॥

तुम्हरी शरण गहूँ मैं बाबा। दुखियों का तुम सहारा बाबा॥
सब जग से जो निराश हो आवे। बाबा शरण में शीश नवावे॥ १३॥

सदा सहारा दे तुम उनको। कभी नहीं ठुकराओ जन को॥
भोजन भी तुम भक्तों को देते। प्रेम प्रीत से सबको जीते॥ १४॥

नवधा भक्ति सिखाई सबको। दान धर्म का पाठ पढ़ाया॥
गुरु महिमा तुम करो बखानी। श्रद्धा सबुरी का मर्म जानी॥ १५॥

तुम समर्थ हो सब विधि बाबा। तुम्हरी कृपा से सब कुछ पाया॥
साईं तुम्हें प्रणाम हमारा। तुम हो हमारा रखवारा॥ १६॥

जो ये चालीसा भक्ति से गावे। सकल मनोरथ पूर्ण पावे॥
साईं बाबा की जय हो सदा। भक्तों को मिले सुख सम्पदा॥ १७॥

नित्य पाठ जो कोई करता। भवसागर से वो तर जाता॥
साईं चरण में ध्यान लगाओ। जीवन में सच्चा सुख पाओ॥ १८॥

सत्य प्रेम का मार्ग बताया। बाबा ने सबको सिखलाया॥
जात पांत से ऊपर उठकर। सबको गले से लगाया मिलकर॥ १९॥

साईं बाबा सबके परमेश्वर। सकल लोक के हैं ईश्वर॥
दया दृष्टि सब पर बरसाओ। हम सबके सब कष्ट मिटाओ॥ २०॥

॥ दोहा ॥

साईं चालीसा पढ़े जो कोई।
उसकी बिगड़ी बने सँवर जोई॥
मन कर्म वचन साईं को ध्यावे।
जीवन में सुख शान्ति पावे॥

॥ इति श्री साई चालीसा सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sai Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||

Pratham Sai ke charan mein, karta mastak tek |
Sakal manorath purna kar, rakhna apni shek ||

Jai Sai jai Sai jai, karuna-sagar Sai |
Bhaktan hit avatar liya, sab sukh det sadai ||

|| Chaupai ||

Shirdi nagar viraje Sai, kripa karo dayanidhi bhai |
Charan sharan mein hamein rakho tum, prem bhakti se tumhein bhajun main || 1 ||

Patit pavan tum ho data, sakal jagat ke ho vidhata |
Bhakta hetu tum Shirdi aaye, chamatkar bahu dikhlaaye || 2 ||

Jal ko paltat tel banaya, acharaj sabne bahut manaya |
Bhakton ko tum darshan dete, unke sakal kasht har lete || 3 ||

Shraddha Saburi batai tumne, do shabdon mein gyan sikhaya |
Karuna nidhi tum dindayalu, bhakta hetu sada pratipalu || 4 ||

Dwarakamai tumhara vasa, purna karo sabki man asha |
Dhuni Sai tumhari jalti, vipada bhaktan ki har balti || 5 ||

Muslaman kah jo tum pukare, Hindu kahkar jo tumhein nihare |
Donon ki rakhwari karte, bhed bhav se kabhi na darte || 6 ||

Tum ho sabke sachche Sai, nirdhan ho ya dhanvan bhai |
Baba tumhein manaun kaise, prem prit ka dan do aise || 7 ||

Sai tumhara hi asra hai, jag mein tum sa nahin koi khara hai |
Jo tere charnon mein aaye, vo kabhi khali nahin jaye || 8 ||

Maan apman tumhein nahin Baba, jag mein tera adbhut taba |
Kushtha rog jinko ho jaye, Baba teri udi lagaye || 9 ||

Sab rogon se mukti ho jaye, Baba ki kripa barsaye |
Bhima Bai ki seva kinha, tahi kanchan tan dinha || 10 ||

Rog mitaye bhaktan ke tum, dukh daridra bhagaye sab tum |
Murali manohar roop tumhara, godhan charavat ati pyara || 11 ||

Bhakton ko darshan de jaate, apne prem mein sabko bandhe |
Baba tum ho antaryami, purna Brahma Paramatma Swami || 12 ||

Tumhari sharan gahun main Baba, dukhiyon ka tum sahara Baba |
Sab jag se jo nirash ho aave, Baba sharan mein shish navave || 13 ||

Sada sahara de tum unko, kabhi nahin thukrao jan ko |
Bhojan bhi tum bhakton ko dete, prem prit se sabko jeete || 14 ||

Navdha bhakti sikhai sabko, dan dharma ka path padhaya |
Guru mahima tum karo bakhani, shraddha saburi ka marm jani || 15 ||

Tum samarth ho sab vidhi Baba, tumhari kripa se sab kuchh paya |
Sai tumhein pranam hamara, tum ho hamara rakhwara || 16 ||

Jo ye chalisa bhakti se gave, sakal manorath purna pave |
Sai Baba ki jai ho sada, bhakton ko mile sukh sampada || 17 ||

Nitya path jo koi karta, bhavsagar se vo tar jata |
Sai charan mein dhyan lagao, jivan mein sachcha sukh pao || 18 ||

Satya prem ka marg bataya, Baba ne sabko sikhaya |
Jat pant se upar uthkar, sabko gale se lagaya milkar || 19 ||

Sai Baba sabke Parmeshwar, sakal lok ke hain Ishwar |
Daya drishti sab par barsao, ham sabke sab kasht mitao || 20 ||

|| Doha ||

Sai chalisa padhe jo koi |
Uski bigdi bane sanvar joi ||
Man karma vachan Sai ko dhyave |
Jivan mein sukh shanti pave ||

|| Iti Shri Sai Chalisa Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: शिर्डी साईं बाबा की स्तुति में रचित यह चालीसा उनकी दिव्य लीलाओं, करुणा और कृपा का वर्णन करती है। साईं बाबा ने श्रद्धा और सबूरी (विश्वास और धैर्य) का मार्ग सिखाया। उन्होंने जल को तेल में बदला, अपनी ऊदी से रोगियों को ठीक किया, और सभी धर्मों के लोगों को समान प्रेम दिया। यह चालीसा भक्तों के कष्ट निवारण और मनोकामना पूर्ति के लिए पढ़ी जाती है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for fulfillment of heartfelt desires through sai baba's grace
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of troubles and suffering from life
Associated in tradition with physical and mental ailments
Promotes communal harmony and universal brotherhood
Invokes the blessing of peace, prosperity and spiritual contentment
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Sai Chalisa (श्री साई चालीसा) is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Shree Sai Baba of Shirdi. Drawn from Traditional Sai Bhakti Literature, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to fulfill all heartfelt desires through Sai Baba's grace as well as bestow peace, prosperity and spiritual contentment. Additionally, it is known to promote communal harmony and universal brotherhood. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima to invoke divine grace. The text contains 20 verses and takes around 15 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Sai Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Sai Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Powerful 40-verse prayer to Shirdi Sai Baba for blessings and wish fulfillment.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sai Chalisa and who is it dedicated to?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sai Chalisa is a devotional hymn of approximately forty verses (chalisa = forty in Hindi) dedicated to Shirdi Sai Baba (c. 1838-1918), the revered fakir-saint of Shirdi village in Maharashtra, India. Sai Baba taught universal love, charity, contentment, and devotion to one God beyond religious labels, immortalised in his teaching 'Sabka Malik Ek' (One God governs all). The chalisa narrates his appearance, miracles, teachings, and grace, and is recited daily by millions of devotees worldwide. (Source: Bhakti Marg; Hindupedia Shirdi Sai Baba Chalisa; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Sai Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sai Chalisa as it is commonly chanted today was compiled in the decades following Sai Baba's mahasamadhi in 1918, drawing on incidents recorded in the Sri Sai Satcharitra by Govind Raghunath Dabholkar (Hemadpant). Multiple recensions exist in Hindi, Marathi, Telugu, and Gujarati, all preserving the central narrative arc of Baba's life at Shirdi. Unlike the Hanuman Chalisa, which has a single famous author (Tulsidas), the Sai Chalisa belongs to a more collective devotional authorship typical of modern bhakti movements. (Source: Sri Sai Satcharitra; Wordzz.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Thursday considered Sai Baba's day?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is universally observed as Sai Baba's day because Baba himself is regarded by devotees as their Sadguru. Thursday evening aarti at Shirdi's Samadhi Mandir, originally Dwarakamai mosque-shrine where Baba lived, draws thousands of devotees. Households worldwide perform Sai puja on Thursdays, light a diya, offer udi (sacred ash), recite the Sai Chalisa, and listen to or read a chapter of the Sai Satcharitra. Many devotees observe a partial fast on Thursdays as a vrata to Baba. (Source: Hindupedia; Bhaktinidhi; Shirdi Sansthan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the prescribed benefits of reciting the Sai Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Devotees and traditional sources state that disciplined recitation of the Sai Chalisa removes worries and obstacles, brings peace and prosperity, restores health when ordinary remedies fail, mends broken relationships, and grants the inner clarity Baba is famous for bestowing. Many begin a 7-Thursday or 9-Thursday parayana for specific intentions, accompanied by reading 7 chapters of the Sai Satcharitra each Thursday until completion. The principle Baba emphasised, 'Shraddha (faith) and Saburi (patience),' is the prescribed inner attitude during recitation. (Source: Sri Mandir; Wordzz; Hindu Gallery.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Sai Chalisa structured?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sai Chalisa opens with an invocation (doha) seeking Baba's blessings, then proceeds through forty chaupais (four-line verses) describing his arrival at Shirdi, his life under the neem tree at Dwarakamai, his miracles (lighting lamps with water, healing plagues, materialising food for visitors), his core teachings, and the ongoing experience of his presence after mahasamadhi. The chalisa concludes with a doha asking Baba to dwell forever in the devotee's heart. The compact form makes it suitable for daily recitation in roughly 8-10 minutes. (Source: Wordzz; Bhakti Marg; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is Shirdi and how does the chalisa relate to its temples?
seoFaqs.answer: Shirdi is a small town in the Ahmednagar district of Maharashtra, India, where Sai Baba lived for approximately sixty years until his mahasamadhi in October 1918. The principal sites include Samadhi Mandir (housing Baba's tomb), Dwarakamai (the mosque-shrine where Baba lived and kept the dhuni or sacred fire still burning today), Chavadi (where he slept on alternate nights), and Lendi Baug (his garden). Devotees who cannot travel to Shirdi recite the Sai Chalisa as a means of mental pilgrimage, since Baba famously promised that he would respond from his samadhi to anyone who calls him sincerely. (Source: Shirdi Sansthan; Hindupedia.)
